name: MetaboBrief starter SNP panel
license: MIT for panel structure; external sources retain their own terms
sourcePolicy: Panel records summarize public scientific sources and are not clinical classifications.

variants.label: MTHFR C677T one-carbon metabolism marker

CC.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as an MTHFR C677T constraint in this starter panel.

CT.interpretation: One T allele may modestly reduce MTHFR enzyme activity. Treat this as a pathway hypothesis and validate with biomarkers rather than acting on genotype alone.

TT.interpretation: Two T alleles are commonly associated with lower MTHFR enzyme activity and higher homocysteine tendency in some contexts. This is not a diagnosis.

limitations: Folate status, B12 status, riboflavin, diet, kidney function, medications, and ancestry affect interpretation.
This panel does not infer folate need or recommend supplementation.

variants.label: MTHFR A1298C supporting one-carbon marker

AA.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as an MTHFR A1298C constraint in this starter panel.

AC.interpretation: One C allele is tracked as a low-attention supporting marker. Interpret together with measured folate-cycle biomarkers.

CC.interpretation: Two C alleles are tracked as a moderate supporting marker. This should not be interpreted without biomarker context.

limitations: A1298C has weaker standalone interpretability than C677T.
This panel does not model compound MTHFR diplotypes beyond simple per-SNP scoring.

variants.label: CYP1A2 caffeine metabolism tendency marker

AA.interpretation: Often discussed as a higher-inducibility CYP1A2 genotype in caffeine studies. Lifestyle, smoking, medications, and hormones can dominate genotype.

AC.interpretation: One C allele is tracked as a possible lower caffeine-clearance tendency. Validate against actual caffeine tolerance and context.

CC.interpretation: Two C alleles are tracked as a possible lower caffeine-clearance tendency. This is not a medication or diet recommendation.

limitations: Caffeine response is strongly affected by dose, timing, smoking, pregnancy, hormones, medications, and sleep debt.
This is not a clinical pharmacogenomic interpretation.

variants.label: Lactase persistence marker
variants.evidenceLevel: established trait marker in some ancestries

CC.interpretation: In many European-ancestry studies, CC is associated with lactase non-persistence tendency. Symptoms and ancestry context matter.

CT.interpretation: One T allele is commonly associated with lactase persistence in European-ancestry contexts.

TT.interpretation: Two T alleles are commonly associated with lactase persistence in European-ancestry contexts.

validationMarkers: Hydrogen breath test if clinically indicated
limitations: Other lactase persistence variants exist and vary by ancestry.
Digestive symptoms can reflect many non-genetic causes.

variants.label: ALDH2 acetaldehyde metabolism marker

GG.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as reduced ALDH2 activity in this starter panel.

AG.interpretation: One A allele is associated with reduced ALDH2 activity and acetaldehyde handling. Avoid interpreting this as permission or prohibition advice.

AA.interpretation: Two A alleles are associated with strongly reduced ALDH2 activity. This is a safety-relevant genotype and should be interpreted conservatively.

limitations: Alcohol response and risk depend on behavior, dose, ancestry, medications, and health context.
This report does not provide alcohol-use advice.

variants.label: Glucose regulation association marker

CC.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as a TCF7L2 T-allele signal in this starter panel.

CT.interpretation: One T allele is a common association signal for glucose-regulation traits. It is not diagnostic.

TT.interpretation: Two T alleles are a stronger common association signal for glucose-regulation traits. Use measured biomarkers for interpretation.

validationMarkers: Oral glucose tolerance test if clinically indicated
limitations: Common-trait associations have small effects and are not deterministic.
Diet, activity, sleep, body composition, medications, and ancestry can dominate genotype.

variants.label: Body-weight and appetite association marker

TT.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as an FTO A-allele signal in this starter panel.

AT.interpretation: One A allele is a common association signal for body-weight and appetite-related traits. It is not deterministic.

AA.interpretation: Two A alleles are a stronger common association signal for body-weight and appetite-related traits. Context matters more than genotype alone.

limitations: FTO associations are population-level signals with modest individual predictive value.
This panel does not estimate obesity risk.

variants.label: HFE C282Y iron-handling marker

GG.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as carrying the C282Y alternate allele in this starter panel.

AG.interpretation: One A allele indicates C282Y carrier status in this simplified panel. Interpret with iron labs and clinical context.

AA.interpretation: Two A alleles are a clinically relevant HFE signal. This requires measured iron markers and qualified clinical review.

limitations: This panel does not diagnose hereditary hemochromatosis.
Penetrance varies, and iron markers are required for practical interpretation.

variants.label: HFE H63D iron-handling marker

CC.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as carrying the H63D alternate allele in this starter panel.

CG.interpretation: One G allele indicates H63D carrier status in this simplified panel. Interpret together with C282Y and measured iron labs.

GG.interpretation: Two G alleles are tracked as an iron-handling signal, but interpretation requires iron labs and clinical context.

limitations: This panel does not phase HFE variants or infer compound heterozygosity.
Measured iron markers are required for practical interpretation.

variants.label: UGT1A1 bilirubin-tendency marker

CC.interpretation: This genotype is not scored as a T-allele bilirubin-tendency signal in this starter panel.

CT.interpretation: One T allele is associated with higher bilirubin tendency in population studies. This is not a liver-disease diagnosis.

TT.interpretation: Two T alleles are associated with a stronger higher-bilirubin tendency. Interpret with measured bilirubin and clinical context.

limitations: Bilirubin is affected by fasting, illness, liver health, hemolysis, medications, and other UGT1A1 variants.
This marker is not a diagnosis of Gilbert syndrome.
